articles.title: Investors rush in hope that War resolution isn't an April Fool's – North American Mid-Week Market Update
articles.expand: Mid-Week update for North-American Markets – The narrative has changed for Markets, easing risk-sentiment for Stock Indexes and dragging both the US Dollar and Canadian Dollar. Taking a close look at NA index and currency performance combined with a USD/CAD intraday chart to spot what's next for American Markets.

articles.title: Tech sector rallies: Semiconductors rise while energy struggles
articles.expand: Sector Overview 📈 Technology & Semiconductors: The technology sector, especially semiconductors, led today's market rally. Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) showed brisk gains of 1.03% and 3.65%, respectively. Intel (INTC) stood out with an impressive rise of 8.59%, bolstering investor confidence in the semiconductor space. 📉 Energy Se...

articles.title: Trump via Vance told Iran that he's open to ceasefire if Hormuz opened - report
articles.expand: Reuters is citing a source briefed on the matter: The VP has been talking to 'intermediaries' about the Iran conflict as recently as Tuesday Trump directed Vance to communicate privately that he is open to a ceasefire as long as certain US demands met including reopening Hormuz Vance delivered a 'stern' message that Trump was impatient and warned o...

articles.title: Oil: Price shock impact on US growth – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank economists Bernd Weidensteiner and Christoph Balz assess how the recent surge in Brent and WTI prices affects the US economy. They stress that the United States is structurally less vulnerable than in the 1970s thanks to lower oil intensity and higher domestic production.

articles.title: EIA weekly US crude oil inventories +5451K vs +814K expected
articles.expand: Prior was +6926K Gasoline -586K vs -1876K exp Distillates -2111K vs -586K exp Refinery utilization -0.8% vs +1.5% exp The API data from late yesterday: Crude +10263K Gasoline -3209K Distillates -1040K The big build is negative for oil and it's the second one in a row.That's hard to believe given what's happening in the Middle East. In terms of pric...

articles.title: Gold rises as USD weakens on US-Iran developments despite strong US data
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades with a mild upside bias on Wednesday, building on the previous day's solid gains as optimism grows that the US-Israel war with Iran could end soon.

articles.title: EUR/USD edges higher as easing US-Iran tensions outweigh support from strong US data
articles.expand: EUR/USD extends its advance for a second consecutive day on Wednesday, climbing to one-week highs as improving optimism around the US-Iran war lifts risk sentiment, pushing the Euro (EUR) higher and weighing on the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: ECB: Pre-emptive hikes flagged on inflation risks – ABN AMRO
articles.expand: ABN AMRO's Senior Economist Bill Diviney argues the ECB is likely to raise rates at its April and June meetings, taking the deposit rate to 2.50% to prevent de-anchoring of inflation expectations.

articles.title: US March ISM manufacturing 52.7 vs 52.5 expected
articles.expand: Prior was 52.5 Prices paid 78.3 vs 73.0 expected (Prior was 70.5) Employment 48.7 vs 48.8 prior New orders 53.5 vs 55.8 prior This prices paid number is worrisome but not surprising. US manufacturing spent most of 2025 in contraction. The ISM PMI slipped to 47.9 in December 2025, its lowest level since October 2024 and the third consecutive monthly...

articles.title: US March S&P Global manufacturing PMI 52.3 vs 52.4 prior
articles.expand: Prior was 52.4 Today's March release showed the PMI rising to 52.3 from 51.6 in February, marking the eighth consecutive month above the 50.0 threshold and pointing to a moderate and accelerating pace of expansion. Both output and new orders posted solid gains, supported in part by precautionary safety stock building as firms sought to lock in supp...

articles.title: USDCAD backs off the boil from the recent trend move higher and corrects lower.
articles.expand: The USDCAD extended above a key swing area between 1.3924 and 1.3937 ( see post yesterday ), pushing toward the next upside target zone between 1.3971 and 1.3994. The rally reached a high of 1.3966—just short of that next resistance band—before stalling and rotating back to the downside. By the close, the price had fallen back below the 1.3924–1.39...

articles.title: UK: Growth risks from energy shock – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank’s Chief UK Economist Sanjay Raja warns that the UK faces rising recession risks as higher energy prices hit growth. Using Hamilton-based energy shock measures and probability models, he expects United Kingdom (UK) GDP growth to slow to 0.7%, with downside risks increasing.

articles.title: Gold Rises as Geopolitical Risk Premium Fades
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex Gold prices rose more than 4% on Wednesday, approaching 4,690 USD per ounce amid signs of easing tensions in the Middle East. Expectations of de-escalation could lead to lower oil prices and reduced concerns about further tightening of central bank policy. Donald Trump stated he was ready to end the conflict [&#82...

articles.title: JPY: Energy shock impact seen more contained – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Head of Research Derek Halpenny notes that the Japanese Yen was one of the best G10 performers in March and argues it has better prospects than the Pound.

articles.title: Fed's Musalem: Monetary policy should hold in place for some time
articles.expand: In a speech delivered on Wednesday before a gathering at the American Enterprise Institute in Washington, St. Louis Federal Reserve President Alberto Musalem said that monetary policy is well-positioned and should hold in place for some time, per Reuters.

articles.title: Gold extends gains near two-week highs amid optimism over US-Iran war
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades with a mild upside bias on Wednesday, building on the previous day's solid gains as optimism grows that the US-Israel war with Iran could end soon.

articles.title: Oil: War risk keeps prices elevated – Rabobank
articles.expand: RaboBank's Global Strategist Michael Every highlights that Gulf War 3 and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz have pushed Oil prices about 60% above pre-war levels, with specific products like diesel and jet fuel particularly affected in Asia.

articles.title: Euro comes out swinging: Can the "Trump Reversal" sustain EUR/USD's upside bias?
articles.expand: EUR/USD has surged towards 1.1600 due to "war optimism" following US President Donald Trump's comments and the hawkish stance of the ECB, which has created a yield differential favoring the Euro. The "Trump Reversal" seems to hold the key.

articles.title: BoE's Bailey: Markets getting ahead of themselves by pricing in rate hikes – Reuters
articles.expand: While speaking to Reuters at the Bank of England's (BoE) London headquarters on Reuters, BoE Governor Andrew Bailey argued that markets are getting ahead of themselves by pricing in interest-rate hikes.

articles.title: BoE's Bailey: I still think markets are getting ahead of themselves by pricing rate hikes
articles.expand: If we have prolonged high energy prices and supply disruption, it will strain many countries quite seriously We will have to act if appropriate, but tackling the source of the energy price shock is most important I'm very clear we need to return inflation to target in a way that causes the least damage to growth and jobs We look at inflation expect...

articles.title: BOE Bailey: We will need to act if approriate.
articles.expand: Bank of England Governor Bailey struck a cautious tone, emphasizing risks around inflation, financial stability, and growth, while signaling the need to stay flexible on policy as conditions evolve. We will have to act if appropriate, but tackling source of energy price shock is most important If high energy prices and supply disruptions persist, i...

articles.title: BoJ: Rate hike prospects supported by solid sentiment – ING
articles.expand: ING economist Min Joo Kang notes that Japanese business sentiment remains relatively firm, with the Tankan Survey and Manufacturing PMI both in expansionary territory.

articles.title: EUR/GBP: Upside bias on BoE repricing – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole writes that EUR/GBP volatility has reflected shifting rate differentials, but Bank of England (BoE) pricing may fall faster than for the European Central Bank (ECB) .

articles.title: US Dollar weakens amid US-Iran optimism; USDJPY approaching a key support zone
articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW USD: The US dollar weakened across the board yesterday after Trump basically confirmed the WSJ report in a Truth Social post that he would be open to end the war with Iran without the Strait of Hormuz opening condition. This turned the sentiment around in the markets and the positive mood improved further later in the day when ...

articles.title: Eurozone PMI Manufacturing Finalized at 45-Month High But War-Fueled Inflation Clouds Outlook
articles.expand: Eurozone PMI Manufacturing was finalized at 51.6 in March, up from 50.8, marking a 45-month high and signaling continued, albeit modest, expansion in the sector. However, according to Joe Hayes, Principal Economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, the Middle East war has already begun to leave a clear imprint on the sector. He noted that [&#8...

articles.title: BOJ policymaker Asada: How to deal with stagflation is a hard question for monetary policy
articles.expand: Rising oil prices put upwards pressure on inflation That also weighs on growth, creating a stagflationary trend How to deal with stagflation situation is a hard question for monetary policy In general, policymakers can deal with such a situation with a mix of fiscal and monetary policy But it is hard to control the economy with monetary policy alon...

articles.title: Eurozone March final manufacturing PMI 51.6 vs 51.4 prelim
articles.expand: Prior 50.8 It's a slight upwards nudge to the initial estimate and a minor improvement in manufacturing activity compared to February. That largely thanks to Germany. That saw production and order growth hold steadier in March but there are some key issues to be concerned about. The Middle East conflict is stirring up supply-side disruption in the ...

articles.title: Spain March manufacturing PMI 48.7 vs 50.4 expected
articles.expand: Prior 50.0 Spain's manufacturing sector activity falls back into contraction territory as the Middle East conflict starts to have an impact. The heightened uncertainty raised by the conflict and higher energy prices are feeding into supply-chain disruptions as well as rapidly rising prices for fuel and inputs. And that is resulting in manufacturers...

articles.title: The Middle East Oil Shock Just Rewrote the ECB’s Interest Rate Outlook
articles.expand: It looked like the ECB was finally done with rate hikes, right up until the March inflation data started telling a different story.

articles.title: Australia assessing how to protect Pacific nations from fuel shortages
articles.expand: Pacific nations are not yet suffering large-scale petrol shortages but are already starting to grapple with the economic impacts of the war, with fuel price hikes threatening to drive up inflation.

articles.title: Asia-Pacific markets rebound with Kospi jumping over 6% as Trump says Iran war could end in weeks
articles.expand: Asia-Pacific markets rebounded Wednesday after statements from U.S. President Donald Trump raised hopes that the Iran war could end soon. On Tuesday stateside, Trump said the U.S. could leave Iran in “two or three weeks,” adding “We leave because there’s no reason for us to do this.” U.S. crude futures were up 0.44% at $101.81... The post Asia-Paci...

articles.title: Banning card surcharges will make paying simpler – but not necessarily cheaper
articles.expand: From October, there’ll be no nasty surprise charges when you pay by card. But some shoppers may have to pay more than they do today – here’s why.

articles.title: Fixed rate shock as ANZ move signals more home loan pain is coming
articles.expand: Another big Aussie bank has again ratcheted up its home loan interest rates and there is more bad news coming. The post Fixed rate shock as ANZ move signals more home loan pain is coming appeared first on realestate.com.au .

Highlights - Arafura executes binding agreements with two cornerstone investo...

articles.title: ATO offers tax relief for small businesses hit by Middle East war
articles.expand: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has announced one group of Aussies will receive major tax relief amid the disruptions triggered by the Iran war.

articles.title: Tankan recap - Japan firms resilient now but warn of worsening outlook ahead
articles.expand: Adding some info on the Tankan to the earlier post , via Reuters recap. Summary: Japan Tankan shows large manufacturers at +17 (vs +16 expected), continuing improvement Large non-manufacturers strong at +36 (vs +33 expected) Firms expect conditions to worsen over next three months Inflation expectations rise to record highs (2.6% 1yr, 2.5% 3–5yr) M...

articles.title: Japan PMI slows to 51.6 as cost pressures rise and outlook weakens
articles.expand: Japan’s manufacturing sector remains in expansion but is losing momentum as cost pressures rise and confidence weakens, with the Middle East conflict feeding through into inflation and uncertainty. Summary: Japan manufacturing PMI eased to 51.6 (prev. 53.0), still in expansion Growth slowed but remains second-strongest since mid-2022 New orders, ou...

articles.title: Families brace for soaring bills amid Iran war as Princes becomes latest firm to warn of 'unprecedented pressures' and hikes ALL prices by 5%
articles.expand: The food firm, which produces tinned tuna, blamed the Iranian closure of the Strait of Hormuz for the price hikes.

articles.title: An upbeat end to a chaotic month – North American Session Market Wrap for March 31
articles.expand: March 31st, 2026 North-American Session Recap – Traders finally hear what they wanted as rushed to buy dips in global assets. A month-end exaggeration or a proper rebound? Tomorrow will be key. Check up the latest trends and developments, daily asset and FX performance, what changed fundamentally and what's coming up in the session ahead.

articles.title: Brent-WTI falls to 2026 lows! Oil corrects as War resolution nears – WTI Outlook
articles.expand: WTI Oil Update: Energy markets signal a major shift as the Brent-WTI spread rushes toward pre-war lows, easing the stress seen since the conflict's start. While a China-Pakistan peace plan fuels a 2.50% rally in equities, Crude Oil remains down from its 50% monthly peak as traders price in a potential truce. Explore a multi-timeframe analysis of th...

articles.title: Is risk-on, back on? The AUDUSD is higher but it is not running quickly to the upside.
articles.expand: The AUDUSD typically trades as a classic risk-on/risk-off barometer. When equities are under pressure and yields are pushing higher, the pair tends to drift lower as traders reduce risk exposure. Conversely, when stocks firm and yields ease, AUDUSD often benefits from a more constructive, risk-on backdrop. Today, the pair is higher by about 0.62%, ...

articles.title: USDCHF runs up to test the high from mid-January and channel trend line
articles.expand: In the earlier post and video on the USDCHF, I spoke to the battle going on between the double tops a 0.8012 and the swing area between 0.79778 and 0.7989. The price did hold support near the high of the swing area - keeping the buyers in control. In the prior post, I wrote: A break above 0.8012 would open the door toward the next target near 0.804...

articles.title: Gold (XAU/USD) rallies 3%, eyes acceptance above the $4600/oz handle for bullish momentum to continue
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) is showing a technical recovery, breaking $4,600/oz as escalating Middle East tensions and a shifting correlation with rising Oil prices boost the "inflation hedge" narrative. Will the bullish momentum continue?

articles.title: The GBPUSD gives up it's gains. Looks toward the low for the day/low for the year
articles.expand: The GBPUSD initially rode the wave of dollar selling into the U.S. session, pushing higher as broader USD weakness helped lift the pair. However, that momentum faded quickly, and buying interest dried up, leading to a reversal. The pair has since rotated back toward unchanged on the day, currently trading near 1.3180 after reaching a high of 1.3264...

articles.title: The USDCHF is waffling between close support and a short term ceiling developed this week
articles.expand: The USDCHF has been riding the broader trend of dollar buying, with the technicals lining up firmly in favor of the buyers. Last week, the pair broke above both the 100-day MA (0.7889) and the 200-day MA (0.7945), and also pushed through a downward-sloping trendline going back to November 2025. That combination helped shift the longer-term bias mor...

articles.title: USDCAD moved above a swing area and increases the bullish bias.
articles.expand: The USDCAD is stretching higher, and in the process has moved back above a key swing area between 1.3924 and 1.3937. That break tilts the bias more to the upside, with both price action and technicals aligned in favor of the buyers. Fundamentally, Canadian GDP came in slightly stronger than expected, which may be offering some support, but this mov...
